brief: Implement `Select` for the UpSkill design system: a labelled dropdown for choosing exactly one option from a predefined list, exported from Select/index.tsx. Requirements: a required `label` (with an option to hide it visually while keeping it accessible); `options` as an array of { value, label }; optional placeholder and error message; supports BOTH uncontrolled (default value) and controlled selected value, notifying the parent whenever the selection changes; the trigger shows a chevron Icon that inherits the trigger's current text color; spread remaining native attributes onto the root element, but consumers must never be able to pass the native change handler — make that a TypeScript error. Style with a co-located CSS module (Select/Select.module.css) referencing only design-token custom properties. Also produce Select/Select.metadata.json that validates against the provided component.schema.json, and a behavioral a11y test Select/Select.a11y.test.tsx (Vitest + Testing Library + vitest-axe, axe's color-contrast rule disabled).

requiredPatterns.reason: canonical controlled-selection change-callback name in this system
narrowed native-attribute type excluding the native change handler

forbiddenPatterns.reason: public onChange prop declaration — the system's Select excludes it via Omit and exposes onValueChange
